data.narrative:
Proteus Space is a Series A stage company in Space & Aerospace founded by David Kervin and Andrew Shapiro. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 public equities (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, with 4 directly applicable private comps at the same or adjacent stage.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $17M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A to next-stage advancement, implying $34M valuation. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies yields a $400M median, with the sample range spanning $274M (Sift, Series B) to $1.3B (Aalyria, Series B). The peer-median method carries greater weight given the direct stage and sector alignment of the comparables. Public-comp anchoring using the Space & Aerospace sector P/S median of 53.2x cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures.

The triangulated valuation range is $29M (floor, stage-progression floor) to $460M (peer-median ceiling), with a median of $217M across the two methods. Primary uncertainty stems from the wide dispersion among private peer valuations, reflecting heterogeneous business models and technology maturity within Space & Aerospace. Stage proximity of ClearSpace ($400M at Series A) provides the most direct comparability.
